metadata.name: Service is missing user
metadata.description: A Compose service without an explicit user commonly runs as root inside the container.
metadata.mitigation: Set a dedicated non-root user for the service container.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose least-privilege guidance; CIS Docker Benchmark recommendations.

metadata.name: Service runs as root
metadata.description: Running a service container as root increases the blast radius of service compromise and container breakout scenarios.
metadata.mitigation: Configure the service to run as a dedicated non-root user.
metadata.reference: Container least-privilege guidance; NIST container security recommendations.

metadata.name: Privileged service enabled
metadata.description: Privileged containers effectively disable most runtime isolation controls and expose host-level attack paths.
metadata.mitigation: Disable privileged mode and grant only the minimal required permissions or capabilities.
metadata.reference: Docker and Compose least-privilege guidance; CIS Docker Benchmark privileged container controls.

metadata.name: read_only is not enabled
metadata.description: Writable root filesystems increase persistence and tampering opportunities inside the running container.
metadata.mitigation: Enable read_only and mount only the minimal writable paths required by the service.
metadata.reference: Container hardening guidance for immutable runtime filesystems.

metadata.name: Service uses host network mode
metadata.description: Host networking removes network namespace isolation and can expose host interfaces and services directly.
metadata.mitigation: Use isolated user-defined networks and publish only the required ports explicitly.
metadata.reference: Docker networking isolation guidance; CIS Docker Benchmark network namespace recommendations.

metadata.name: Service shares host PID namespace
metadata.description: Sharing the host PID namespace exposes host process visibility and increases the impact of compromise.
metadata.mitigation: Run the service in its own PID namespace unless a documented operational need requires otherwise.
metadata.reference: Docker runtime isolation guidance for namespace separation.

metadata.name: Service shares host IPC namespace
metadata.description: Sharing IPC with the host weakens isolation and can expose inter-process communication primitives.
metadata.mitigation: Keep the service in an isolated IPC namespace.
metadata.reference: Docker runtime isolation guidance for IPC namespace separation.

metadata.name: cap_drop does not include ALL
metadata.description: Services that do not drop broad capability sets often retain more kernel privileges than necessary.
metadata.mitigation: Drop all capabilities by default and add back only narrowly required permissions.
metadata.reference: Docker capability hardening guidance; least-privilege container runtime practices.

metadata.name: security_opt misses no-new-privileges
metadata.description: Without no-new-privileges, child processes may still gain additional privileges through setuid or similar mechanisms.
metadata.mitigation: Set security_opt: ["no-new-privileges:true"] for the service.
metadata.reference: Docker security options guidance; container privilege escalation hardening practices.

metadata.name: Insecure security_opt detected
metadata.description: Options such as unconfined or disabled labels weaken or bypass important runtime confinement controls.
metadata.mitigation: Remove insecure security_opt values and keep mandatory access control profiles enabled.
metadata.reference: Docker security options documentation; MAC/LSM container hardening guidance.

metadata.name: Service maps host devices
metadata.description: Direct host device access broadens the service attack surface and may expose sensitive hardware interfaces.
metadata.mitigation: Remove host device mappings or isolate the workload onto a dedicated trusted runtime boundary.
metadata.reference: Docker device mapping guidance and runtime least-privilege recommendations.

metadata.name: Secret-like data in environment
metadata.description: Secrets in environment variables are easily exposed through process inspection, logs, or diagnostics.
metadata.mitigation: Move sensitive values to dedicated Compose secrets or another secure secret delivery mechanism.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose secrets guidance; NIST container secret management recommendations.

metadata.name: Missing healthcheck
metadata.description: Services without a healthcheck are harder to monitor and recover automatically when degraded.
metadata.mitigation: Add a meaningful healthcheck that reflects service readiness or liveness.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose healthcheck documentation and operational resilience guidance.

metadata.name: depends_on lacks service_healthy condition
metadata.description: Starting dependent services without health-based conditions can create fragile startup ordering and runtime errors.
metadata.mitigation: Use health-based dependency conditions where service readiness matters.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose depends_on and healthcheck coordination guidance.

metadata.name: Missing restart policy
metadata.description: Services without restart behavior configured may remain down after transient failures or host restarts.
metadata.mitigation: Set an appropriate restart policy for the service operational model.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose restart policy guidance for resilient service operation.

metadata.name: Unsafe restart policy
metadata.description: A no restart policy leaves the service unavailable after failures unless manually recovered.
metadata.mitigation: Use a restart policy such as unless-stopped or on-failure where appropriate.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose operational resiliency guidance for restart handling.

metadata.name: Insecure user namespace mode
metadata.description: Host-like or shared user namespace modes reduce user isolation and can weaken runtime separation.
metadata.mitigation: Avoid host or shared user namespace modes unless there is a documented and justified need.
metadata.reference: Docker user namespace remapping guidance and container isolation best practices.

metadata.name: Capability additions are enabled
metadata.description: Adding Linux capabilities increases kernel-level privileges beyond the safer default profile.
metadata.mitigation: Remove cap_add entries unless each added capability is explicitly justified.
metadata.reference: Docker capability model guidance; least-privilege container runtime practices.

metadata.name: Dangerous capability added
metadata.description: Capabilities such as SYS_ADMIN, NET_ADMIN, and SYS_PTRACE provide especially broad or sensitive host interaction powers.
metadata.mitigation: Remove dangerous capability additions and redesign the service to operate with a narrower privilege set.
metadata.reference: Docker capability hardening guidance; CIS Docker Benchmark capability recommendations.

metadata.name: Docker socket is mounted from host
metadata.description: Mounting docker.sock exposes the Docker daemon API and effectively grants host-level control to the container.
metadata.mitigation: Remove the docker.sock mount or separate build/orchestration duties from the application container.
metadata.reference: Docker daemon attack surface guidance; container breakout risk documentation.

metadata.name: Sensitive host path is mounted
metadata.description: Mounting host paths such as /proc, /sys, /etc, or / exposes sensitive host state and configuration to the container.
metadata.mitigation: Remove broad host path mounts and replace them with narrowly scoped data volumes or dedicated APIs.
metadata.reference: Docker bind mount security guidance; host access minimization practices.

metadata.name: Secret-like environment is used without compose secrets
metadata.description: Services carrying secret-like environment data without Compose secrets use a weaker secret distribution model.
metadata.mitigation: Define the secret at the Compose top level and mount it through service.secrets instead of environment variables.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose secrets documentation; secure secret handling guidance.

metadata.name: Resource limits or reservations are missing
metadata.description: Services without resource constraints can affect availability by exhausting shared host resources.
metadata.mitigation: Define resource limits and, where appropriate, reservations for CPU and memory.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose deploy.resources guidance; container resource governance recommendations.

metadata.name: Service publishes ports publicly
metadata.description: Publicly bound service ports expand external attack surface and can expose internal services directly.
metadata.mitigation: Bind only to loopback or place the service on an internal network behind a dedicated ingress or reverse proxy.
metadata.reference: Compose network hardening guidance; secure service exposure practices.

metadata.name: Debug-like service is active without profiles
metadata.description: Debug, admin, or developer-oriented services should not be active in normal deployments by default.
metadata.mitigation: Put debug-like services behind Compose profiles and activate them only when explicitly needed.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose profiles documentation and environment separation practices.

metadata.name: Service has build context but no image
metadata.description: Deployment-oriented Compose definitions should identify the runtime image explicitly rather than relying only on local build context.
metadata.mitigation: Specify an explicit image reference for the service and use build only where that workflow is intentionally required.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose build and image guidance for deployable service definitions.

metadata.name: Service image uses latest tag
metadata.description: Mutable latest tags reduce deployment repeatability and complicate rollout review.
metadata.mitigation: Use explicit version tags and preferably immutable digests for deployed service images.
metadata.reference: Docker image pinning guidance; reproducible deployment best practices.

metadata.name: Service has no explicit network segmentation
metadata.description: Relying only on the default network weakens intentional service segmentation and policy clarity.
metadata.mitigation: Attach services to explicit user-defined networks that reflect trust boundaries and communication intent.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose networking guidance for service segmentation and isolation.

metadata.name: Logging policy is not configured
metadata.description: Services without explicit logging settings may not align with retention, routing, or operational observability requirements.
metadata.mitigation: Define a logging driver and options appropriate for the deployment environment.
metadata.reference: Docker logging driver documentation and operational observability guidance.

metadata.name: Init process is not enabled
metadata.description: Without an init process, long-running containers may not reap zombie processes or handle signals cleanly.
metadata.mitigation: Enable init for long-running services unless process handling is managed explicitly by the application.
metadata.reference: Docker init process guidance for container process lifecycle handling.

metadata.name: Stop controls are not configured
metadata.description: Missing stop signal and grace period settings can lead to abrupt shutdown behavior and incomplete cleanup.
metadata.mitigation: Set stop_signal and or stop_grace_period to match the service shutdown behavior.
metadata.reference: Docker Compose stop lifecycle configuration guidance for graceful termination.
